title: buildah volume names ignored in podman
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

volumes created in 'buildah config -v' are created in podman during 'podman run' but the names assigned in buildah to the image are ignored.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. buildah from $image --name $yourimage

2. buildah config -v test:/path/to/bind/mount $yourimage

3.buildah commit 

**Describe the results you received:**
podman volume ls shows volumes are created and tracked properly, however 
the names are 65 character anonymous bind...not the name set in buildah. 

**Describe the results you expected:**
names set for mount points in buildah are honored by podman

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

RemoteAPI Version:  1

Git Commit:         b7ce1157b00af09f4a09e39b377aa3abff46ee05-dirty

comments.body: I don't believe that names for volumes are part of the OCI or Docker image specifications, just the paths they will be made at - @baude @vrothberg correct me if I'm wrong here

ah, silly me.  this isnt part of the spec at all and for good reason.
https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/30647

spec 1.1 and 1.2 also do not reference named volumes anywhere...so i wonder why buildah even offers the option to name a volume if its pointless in the image
